heading: Distribution of stock and securities of a controlled corporation
label: 355 Distribution of stock and securities of a controlled corporation
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this subsection, referred to in subsec. (g)(2)(A)(i), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 109–222May 17, 2006

(g). , added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 109–222, § 507(a)1998—Subsec. (e)(3)(A). , substituted “shall not be taken into account in applying” for “shall not be treated as described in” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–206, § 6010(c)(2)(A)Subsec. (e)(3)(A)(iv). , added cl. (iv) and struck out former cl. (iv) which read as follows: “The acquisition of stock in a corporation if shareholders owning directly or indirectly stock possessing—Pub. (a)(3)(C). , inserted “(including nonqualified preferred stock, as defined in section 351(g)(2))” after “stock”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1014(e)(1)Subsec. (a)(3)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1014(c)Subsec. (a)(4)(A). , inserted “nonqualified preferred stock and” after “subsection (including”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1014(e)(2)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1012(a)Subsec. (f). , added subsec. (f).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1012(b)(1)1996—Subsec. (d)(7)(A). inserted “section” before “267(b)”.Pub. L. 104–1881990—Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which read as follows:Pub. L. 101–508, § 11321(a)“(1) .—Except as provided in paragraph (2), no gain or loss shall be recognized to a corporation on any distribution to which this section (or so much of section 356 as relates to this section) applies and which is not in pursuance of a plan of reorganization.In general“(2)Distribution of appreciated property.—“(A) .—If—In general“(i) in a distribution referred to in paragraph (1), the corporation distributes property other than stock or securities in the controlled corporation, and“(ii) the fair market value of such property exceeds its adjusted basis (in the hands of the distributing corporation),then gain shall be recognized to the distributing corporation as if such property were sold to the distributee at its fair market value.“(B) .—If any property distributed in the distribution referred to in paragraph (1) is subject to a liability or the shareholder assumes a liability of the distributing corporation in connection with the distribution, then, for purposes of subparagraph (A), the fair market value of such property shall be treated as not less than the amount of such liability.Treatment of liabilities“(3) (a).—Sections 311 and 336(a) shall not apply to any distribution referred to in paragraph (1).”Coordination with sections 311 and 336, amended subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (c) read as follows: “Section 311 shall apply to any distribution—Pub. L. 101–508, § 11702(e)(2)“(1) to which this section (or so much of section 356 as relates to this section) applies, and“(2) which is not in pursuance of a plan of reorganization,in the same manner as if such distribution were a distribution to which subpart A of part I applies; except that subsection (b) of section 311 shall not apply to any distribution of stock or securities in the controlled corporation.”Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 101–508, § 11321(a)1988—Subsec. (b)(2)(D)(i), (ii). , added cls. (i) and (ii) and struck out former cls. (i) and (ii) which read as follows:Pub. L. 100–647, § 2004(k)(1)“(i) was not acquired by any distributee corporation directly (or through 1 or more corporations, whether through the distributing corporation or otherwise) within the period described in subparagraph (B), or“(ii) was so acquired such distributee corporation within such period, but such control was so acquired only by reason of transactions in which gain or loss was not recognized in whole or in part, or only by reason of such transactions combined with acquisitions before the beginning of such period.”Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 100–647, § 1018(d)(5)(C)1987—Subsec. (b)(2)(D). , inserted at end “For purposes of subparagraph (D), all distributee corporations which are members of the same affiliated group (as defined in section 1504(a) without regard to section 1504(b)) shall be treated as 1 distributee corporation.”Pub. L. 100–203, § 10223(b)(3)Subsec. (b)(2)(D)(i). , amended cl. (i) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (i) read as follows: “was not acquired directly (or through one or more corporations) by another corporation within the period described in subparagraph (B), or”.Pub. L. 100–203, § 10223(b)(1)Subsec. (b)(2)(D)(ii). , substituted “such distributee corporation” for “by another corporation”.Pub. L. 100–203, § 10223(b)(2)1980—Subsec. (a)(3). designated existing provisions as subpars. (A) and (B) and added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 96–589Subsec. (a)(4). , designated existing provisions as subpar. (A), substituted “exchange if any property” for “distribution if any property”, inserted provisions excluding property to which paragraph (3)(C) applies, and added subpar. (B).Pub. L. 96–589, § 4(e)(2)1976—Subsec. (a)(1)(D)(ii). struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455

Effective Date of 2015 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–113, div. Q, title III, § 311(c)129 Stat. 3091Dec. 18, 2015“The amendments made by this section [amending this section and ] shall apply to distributions on or after , but shall not apply to any distribution pursuant to a transaction described in a ruling request initially submitted to the Internal Revenue Service on or before such date, which request has not been withdrawn and with respect to which a ruling has not been issued or denied in its entirety as of such date.”section 856 of this titleDecember 7, 2015

Effective Date of 2014 AmendmentAmendment by effective , subject to a savings provision, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 113–295section 221(b) of Pub. L. 113–295section 1 of this titleDec. 19, 2014

Effective Date of 2007 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–172, § 4(d)121 Stat. 2478Dec. 29, 2007“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 911 and 954 of this title] shall take effect as if included in the provisions of the Tax Increase Prevention and Reconciliation Act of 2005 [] to which they relate.Pub. L. 109–222“(2)Modification of active business definition under section 355.—“(A).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this paragraph, the amendments made by subsection (b) [amending this section] shall apply to distributions made after .May 17, 2006“(B).—Transition ruleThe amendments made by subsection (b) shall not apply to any distribution pursuant to a transaction which is—“(i)made pursuant to an agreement which was binding on , and at all times thereafter,May 17, 2006“(ii)described in a ruling request submitted to the Internal Revenue Service on or before such date, or“(iii)described on or before such date in a public announcement or in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.“(C).—Election out of transition ruleSubparagraph (B) shall not apply if the distributing corporation elects not to have such subparagraph apply to distributions of such corporation. Any such election, once made, shall be irrevocable.“(D).—Special rule for certain pre-enactment distributionsFor purposes of determining the continued qualification under section 355(b)(2)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 of distributions made on or before , as a result of an acquisition, disposition, or other restructuring after such date, such distribution shall be treated as made on the date of such acquisition, disposition, or restructuring for purposes of applying subparagraphs (A) through (C) of this paragraph. The preceding sentence shall only apply with respect to the corporation that undertakes such acquisition, disposition, or other restructuring, and only if such application results in continued qualification under section 355(b)(2)(A) of such Code.May 17, 2006“(3).—Amendment related to section 515 of the actThe amendment made by subsection (c) [amending ] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .”section 911 of this titleDecember 31, 2006

Effective Date of 2006 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–432, div. A, title IV, § 410(b)120 Stat. 2963Dec. 20, 2006“The amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall take effect as if included in section 202 of the Tax Increase Prevention and Reconciliation Act of 2005 [].”Pub. L. 109–222, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–222, title V, § 507(b)120 Stat. 361May 17, 2006“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to distributions after the date of the enactment of this Act [].May 17, 2006“(2).—Transition ruleThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to any distribution pursuant to a transaction which is—“(A)made pursuant to an agreement which was binding on such date of enactment and at all times thereafter,“(B)described in a ruling request submitted to the Internal Revenue Service on or before such date, or“(C)described on or before such date in a public announcement or in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.”

L. 109–222Pub. L. 109–432

children.label: i
children.text: distributes to a shareholder, with respect to its stock, or

children.label: ii
children.text: distributes to a security holder, in exchange for its securities,

children.label: B
children.text: the transaction was not used principally as a device for the distribution of the earnings and profits of the distributing corporation or the controlled corporation or both (but the mere fact that subsequent to the distribution stock or securities in one or more of such corporations are sold or exchanged by all or some of the distributees (other than pursuant to an arrangement negotiated or agreed upon prior to such distribution) shall not be construed to mean that the transaction was used principally as such a device),

children.label: C
children.text: the requirements of subsection (b) (relating to active businesses) are satisfied, and

children.label: i
children.text: all of the stock and securities in the controlled corporation held by it immediately before the distribution, or

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount of stock in the controlled corporation constituting control within the meaning of section 368(c), and it is established to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the retention by the distributing corporation of stock (or stock and securities) in the controlled corporation was not in pursuance of a plan having as one of its principal purposes the avoidance of Federal income tax,

children.label: A
children.text: whether or not the distribution is pro rata with respect to all of the shareholders of the distributing corporation,

children.label: B
children.text: whether or not the shareholder surrenders stock in the distributing corporation, and

children.label: C
children.text: whether or not the distribution is in pursuance of a plan of reorganization (within the meaning of section 368(a)(1)(D)).

children.label: i
children.text: the principal amount of the securities in the controlled corporation which are received exceeds the principal amount of the securities which are surrendered in connection with such distribution, or

children.label: ii
children.text: securities in the controlled corporation are received and no securities are surrendered in connection with such distribution.

children.heading: Stock acquired in taxable transactions within 5 years treated as boot
children.label: B Stock acquired in taxable transactions within 5 years treated as boot
i
children.text: which occurs within 5 years of the distribution of such stock, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in which gain or loss was recognized in whole or in part,

children.heading: Property attributable to accrued interest
children.label: C Property attributable to accrued interest
children.text: Neither paragraph (1) nor so much of section 356 as relates to paragraph (1) shall apply to the extent that any stock (including nonqualified preferred stock, as defined in section 351(g)(2)), securities, or other property received is attributable to interest which has accrued on securities on or after the beginning of the holder’s holding period.

children.heading: Nonqualified preferred stock
children.label: D Nonqualified preferred stock
children.text: Nonqualified preferred stock (as defined in section 351(g)(2)) received in a distribution with respect to stock other than nonqualified preferred stock (as so defined) shall not be treated as stock or securities.

children.label: A
children.text: For treatment of the exchange if any property is received which is not permitted to be received under this subsection (including nonqualified preferred stock and an excess principal amount of securities received over securities surrendered, but not including property to which paragraph (3)(C) applies), see section 356.

children.label: B
children.text: For treatment of accrued interest in the case of an exchange described in paragraph (3)(C), see section 61.

children.heading: Requirements as to active business
children.label: b Requirements as to active business
A
children.text: the distributing corporation, and the controlled corporation (or, if stock of more than one controlled corporation is distributed, each of such corporations), is engaged immediately after the distribution in the active conduct of a trade or business, or

children.label: B
children.text: immediately before the distribution, the distributing corporation had no assets other than stock or securities in the controlled corporations and each of the controlled corporations is engaged immediately after the distribution in the active conduct of a trade or business.

children.label: A
children.text: it is engaged in the active conduct of a trade or business,

children.label: B
children.text: such trade or business has been actively conducted throughout the 5-year period ending on the date of the distribution,

children.label: C
children.text: such trade or business was not acquired within the period described in subparagraph (B) in a transaction in which gain or loss was recognized in whole or in part, and

children.label: i
children.text: was not acquired by any distributee corporation directly (or through 1 or more corporations, whether through the distributing corporation or otherwise) within the period described in subparagraph (B) and was not acquired by the distributing corporation directly (or through 1 or more corporations) within such period, or

children.label: ii
children.text: was so acquired by any such corporation within such period, but, in each case in which such control was so acquired, it was so acquired, only by reason of transactions in which gain or loss was not recognized in whole or in part, or only by reason of such transactions combined with acquisitions before the beginning of such period.

children.heading: Special rules for determining active conduct in the case of affiliated groups
children.label: 3 Special rules for determining active conduct in the case of affiliated groups

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of determining whether a corporation meets the requirements of paragraph (2)(A), all members of such corporation’s separate affiliated group shall be treated as one corporation.

children.heading: Separate affiliated group
children.label: B Separate affiliated group
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “separate affiliated group” means, with respect to any corporation, the affiliated group which would be determined under section 1504(a) if such corporation were the common parent and section 1504(b) did not apply.

children.heading: Treatment of trade or business conducted by acquired member
children.label: C Treatment of trade or business conducted by acquired member
children.text: If a corporation became a member of a separate affiliated group as a result of one or more transactions in which gain or loss was recognized in whole or in part, any trade or business conducted by such corporation (at the time that such corporation became such a member) shall be treated for purposes of paragraph (2) as acquired in a transaction in which gain or loss was recognized in whole or in part.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: D Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as are necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this paragraph, including regulations which provide for the proper application of subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) of paragraph (2), and modify the application of subsection (a)(3)(B), in connection with the application of this paragraph.

children.heading: Taxability of corporation on distribution
children.label: c Taxability of corporation on distribution

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no gain or loss shall be recognized to a corporation on any distribution to which this section (or so much of section 356 as relates to this section) applies and which is not in pursuance of a plan of reorganization.

children.label: i
children.text: in a distribution referred to in paragraph (1), the corporation distributes property other than qualified property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value of such property exceeds its adjusted basis (in the hands of the distributing corporation),

children.heading: Qualified property
children.label: B Qualified property
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “qualified property” means any stock or securities in the controlled corporation.

children.heading: Treatment of liabilities
children.label: C Treatment of liabilities
children.text: If any property distributed in the distribution referred to in paragraph (1) is subject to a liability or the shareholder assumes a liability of the distributing corporation in connection with the distribution, then, for purposes of subparagraph (A), the fair market value of such property shall be treated as not less than the amount of such liability.

children.heading: Coordination with sections 311 and 336(a)
children.label: 3 Coordination with sections 311 and 336(a)
children.text: Sections 311 and 336(a) shall not apply to any distribution referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Recognition of gain on certain distributions of stock or securities in controlled corporation
children.label: d Recognition of gain on certain distributions of stock or securities in controlled corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a disqualified distribution, any stock or securities in the controlled corporation shall not be treated as qualified property for purposes of subsection (c)(2) of this section or section 361(c)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: any person holds disqualified stock in the distributing corporation which constitutes a 50-percent or greater interest in such corporation, or

children.label: B
children.text: any person holds disqualified stock in the controlled corporation (or, if stock of more than 1 controlled corporation is distributed, in any controlled corporation) which constitutes a 50-percent or greater interest in such corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: any stock in the distributing corporation acquired by purchase during the 5-year period ending on the date of the distribution, and

children.label: i
children.text: acquired by purchase during the 5-year period ending on the date of the distribution, or

children.label: I
children.text: stock described in subparagraph (A), or

children.label: II
children.text: any securities in the distributing corporation acquired by purchase during the 5-year period ending on the date of the distribution.

children.heading: 50-percent or greater interest
children.label: 4 50-percent or greater interest
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “50-percent or greater interest” means stock possessing at least 50 percent of the total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote or at least 50 percent of the total value of shares of all classes of stock.

children.label: i
children.text: the basis of the property acquired in the hands of the acquirer is not determined (I) in whole or in part by reference to the adjusted basis of such property in the hands of the person from whom acquired, or (II) under section 1014(a), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the property is not acquired in an exchange to which section 351, 354, 355, or 356 applies.

children.heading: Certain section 351 exchanges treated as purchases
children.label: B Certain section 351 exchanges treated as purchases
i
children.text: any cash or cash item,

children.label: ii
children.text: any marketable stock or security, or

children.label: iii
children.text: any debt of the transferor.

children.label: i
children.text: any person acquires property from another person who acquired such property by purchase (as determined under this paragraph with regard to this subparagraph), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the adjusted basis of such property in the hands of such acquirer is determined in whole or in part by reference to the adjusted basis of such property in the hands of such other person,

children.heading: Special rule where substantial diminution of risk
children.label: 6 Special rule where substantial diminution of risk

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If this paragraph applies to any stock or securities for any period, the running of any 5-year period set forth in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (3) (whichever applies) shall be suspended during such period.

children.heading: Property to which suspension applies
children.label: B Property to which suspension applies
i
children.text: an option,

children.label: ii
children.text: a short sale,

children.label: iii
children.text: any special class of stock, or

children.label: iv
children.text: any other device or transaction.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a person and all persons related to such person (within the meaning of section 267(b) or 707(b)(1)) shall be treated as one person.

children.heading: Persons acting pursuant to plans or arrangements
children.label: B Persons acting pursuant to plans or arrangements
children.text: If two or more persons act pursuant to a plan or arrangement with respect to acquisitions of stock or securities in the distributing corporation or controlled corporation, such persons shall be treated as one person for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Paragraph (2) of section 318(a) shall apply in determining whether a person holds stock or securities in any corporation (determined by substituting “10 percent” for “50 percent” in subparagraph (C) of such paragraph (2) and by treating any reference to stock as including a reference to securities).

children.label: i
children.text: any person acquires by purchase an interest in any entity, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such person is treated under subparagraph (A) as holding any stock or securities by reason of holding such interest,

children.label: A
children.text: regulations to prevent the avoidance of the purposes of this subsection through the use of related persons, intermediaries, pass-thru entities, options, or other arrangements, and

children.label: B
children.text: regulations modifying the definition of the term “purchase”.

children.heading: Recognition of gain on certain distributions of stock or securities in connection with acquisitions
children.label: e Recognition of gain on certain distributions of stock or securities in connection with acquisitions

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: If there is a distribution to which this subsection applies, any stock or securities in the controlled corporation shall not be treated as qualified property for purposes of subsection (c)(2) of this section or section 361(c)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: to which this section (or so much of section 356 as relates to this section) applies, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which is part of a plan (or series of related transactions) pursuant to which 1 or more persons acquire directly or indirectly stock representing a 50-percent or greater interest in the distributing corporation or any controlled corporation.

children.heading: Plan presumed to exist in certain cases
children.label: B Plan presumed to exist in certain cases
children.text: If 1 or more persons acquire directly or indirectly stock representing a 50-percent or greater interest in the distributing corporation or any controlled corporation during the 4-year period beginning on the date which is 2 years before the date of the distribution, such acquisition shall be treated as pursuant to a plan described in subparagraph (A)(ii) unless it is established that the distribution and the acquisition are not pursuant to a plan or series of related transactions.

children.heading: Certain plans disregarded
children.label: C Certain plans disregarded
children.text: A plan (or series of related transactions) shall not be treated as described in subparagraph (A)(ii) if, immediately after the completion of such plan or transactions, the distributing corporation and all controlled corporations are members of a single affiliated group (as defined in section 1504 without regard to subsection (b) thereof).

children.heading: Coordination with subsection (d)
children.label: D Coordination with subsection (d)
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any distribution to which subsection (d) applies.

children.heading: Certain acquisitions not taken into account
children.label: A Certain acquisitions not taken into account
i
children.text: The acquisition of stock in any controlled corporation by the distributing corporation.

children.label: ii
children.text: The acquisition by a person of stock in any controlled corporation by reason of holding stock or securities in the distributing corporation.

children.label: iii
children.text: The acquisition by a person of stock in any successor corporation of the distributing corporation or any controlled corporation by reason of holding stock or securities in such distributing or controlled corporation.

children.label: iv
children.text: The acquisition of stock in the distributing corporation or any controlled corporation to the extent that the percentage of stock owned directly or indirectly in such corporation by each person owning stock in such corporation immediately before the acquisition does not decrease.

children.heading: Asset acquisitions
children.label: B Asset acquisitions
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, for purposes of this subsection, if the assets of the distributing corporation or any controlled corporation are acquired by a successor corporation in a transaction described in subparagraph (A), (C), or (D) of section 368(a)(1) or any other transaction specified in regulations by the Secretary, the shareholders (immediately before the acquisition) of the corporation acquiring such assets shall be treated as acquiring stock in the corporation from which the assets were acquired.

children.heading: 50-percent or greater interest
children.label: A 50-percent or greater interest
children.text: The term “50-percent or greater interest” has the meaning given such term by subsection (d)(4).

children.heading: Distributions in title 11 or similar case
children.label: B Distributions in title 11 or similar case
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any distribution made in a title 11 or similar case (as defined in section 368(a)(3)).

children.heading: Aggregation
children.label: i Aggregation
children.text: The rules of paragraph (7)(A) of subsection (d) shall apply.

children.heading: Attribution
children.label: ii Attribution
children.text: Section 318(a)(2) shall apply in determining whether a person holds stock or securities in any corporation. Except as provided in regulations, section 318(a)(2)(C) shall be applied without regard to the phrase “50 percent or more in value” for purposes of the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Successors and predecessors
children.label: D Successors and predecessors
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, any reference to a controlled corporation or a distributing corporation shall include a reference to any predecessor or successor of such corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: the statutory period for the assessment of any deficiency attributable to any part of the gain recognized under this subsection by reason of such distribution shall not expire before the expiration of 3 years from the date the Secretary is notified by the taxpayer (in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) that such distribution occurred, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such deficiency may be assessed before the expiration of such 3-year period notwithstanding the provisions of any other law or rule of law which would otherwise prevent such assessment.

children.label: A
children.text: providing for the application of this subsection where there is more than 1 controlled corporation,

children.label: B
children.text: treating 2 or more distributions as 1 distribution where necessary to prevent the avoidance of such purposes, and

children.label: C
children.text: providing for the application of rules similar to the rules of subsection (d)(6) where appropriate for purposes of paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: Section not to apply to certain intragroup distributions
children.label: f Section not to apply to certain intragroup distributions
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, this section (or so much of section 356 as relates to this section) shall not apply to the distribution of stock from 1 member of an affiliated group (as defined in section 1504(a)) to another member of such group if such distribution is part of a plan (or series of related transactions) described in subsection (e)(2)(A)(ii) (determined after the application of subsection (e)).

children.heading: Section not to apply to distributions involving disqualified investment corporations
children.label: g Section not to apply to distributions involving disqualified investment corporations
A
children.text: either the distributing corporation or controlled corporation is, immediately after the transaction, a disqualified investment corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: any person holds, immediately after the transaction, a 50-percent or greater interest in any disqualified investment corporation, but only if such person did not hold such an interest in such corporation immediately before the transaction.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of distributions after the end of the 1-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this subsection, ⅔ or more of the fair market value of all assets of the corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of distributions during such 1-year period, ¾ or more of the fair market value of all assets of the corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: cash,

children.label: II
children.text: any stock or securities in a corporation,

children.label: III
children.text: any interest in a partnership,

children.label: IV
children.text: any debt instrument or other evidence of indebtedness,

children.label: V
children.text: any option, forward or futures contract, notional principal contract, or derivative,

children.label: VI
children.text: foreign currency, or

children.label: VII
children.text: any similar asset.

children.heading: Exception for assets used in active conduct of certain financial trades or businesses
children.label: ii Exception for assets used in active conduct of certain financial trades or businesses
I
children.text: a lending or finance business (within the meaning of section 954(h)(4)),

children.label: II
children.text: a banking business through a bank (as defined in section 581), a domestic building and loan association (within the meaning of section 7701(a)(19)), or any similar institution specified by the Secretary, or

children.label: III
children.text: an insurance business if the conduct of the business is licensed, authorized, or regulated by an applicable insurance regulatory body.

children.heading: Exception for securities marked to market
children.label: iii Exception for securities marked to market
children.text: Such term shall not include any security (as defined in section 475(c)(2)) which is held by a dealer in securities and to which section 475(a) applies.

children.heading: Stock or securities in a 20-percent controlled entity
children.label: iv Stock or securities in a 20-percent controlled entity

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Such term shall not include any stock and securities in, or any asset described in subclause (IV) or (V) of clause (i) issued by, a corporation which is a 20-percent controlled entity with respect to the distributing or controlled corporation.

children.heading: Look-thru rule
children.label: II Look-thru rule
children.text: The distributing or controlled corporation shall, for purposes of applying this subsection, be treated as owning its ratable share of the assets of any 20-percent controlled entity.

children.heading: 20-percent controlled entity
children.label: III 20-percent controlled entity
children.text: For purposes of this clause, the term “20-percent controlled entity” means, with respect to any distributing or controlled corporation, any corporation with respect to which the distributing or controlled corporation owns directly or indirectly stock meeting the requirements of section 1504(a)(2), except that such section shall be applied by substituting “20 percent” for “80 percent” and without regard to stock described in section 1504(a)(4).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Such term shall not include any interest in a partnership, or any debt instrument or other evidence of indebtedness, issued by the partnership, if 1 or more of the trades or businesses of the partnership are (or, without regard to the 5-year requirement under subsection (b)(2)(B), would be) taken into account by the distributing or controlled corporation, as the case may be, in determining whether the requirements of subsection (b) are met with respect to the distribution.

children.heading: Look-thru rule
children.label: II Look-thru rule
children.text: The distributing or controlled corporation shall, for purposes of applying this subsection, be treated as owning its ratable share of the assets of any partnership described in subclause (I).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “50-percent or greater interest” has the meaning given such term by subsection (d)(4).

children.heading: Attribution rules
children.label: B Attribution rules
children.text: The rules of section 318 shall apply for purposes of determining ownership of stock for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Transaction
children.label: 4 Transaction
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “transaction” includes a series of transactions.

children.label: i
children.text: the use of related persons, intermediaries, pass-thru entities, options, or other arrangements, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the treatment of assets unrelated to the trade or business of a corporation as investment assets if, prior to the distribution, investment assets were used to acquire such unrelated assets,

children.label: B
children.text: which in appropriate cases exclude from the application of this subsection a distribution which does not have the character of a redemption which would be treated as a sale or exchange under section 302, and

children.label: C
children.text: which modify the application of the attribution rules applied for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Restriction on distributions involving real estate investment trusts
children.label: h Restriction on distributions involving real estate investment trusts

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section (and so much of section 356 as relates to this section) shall not apply to any distribution if either the distributing corporation or controlled corporation is a real estate investment trust.

children.heading: Distributions of a real estate investment trust by another real estate investment trust
children.label: A Distributions of a real estate investment trust by another real estate investment trust
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any distribution if, immediately after the distribution, the distributing corporation and the controlled corporation are both real estate investment trusts.

children.heading: Distributions of certain taxable REIT subsidiaries
children.label: B Distributions of certain taxable REIT subsidiaries
i
children.text: the distributing corporation has been a real estate investment trust at all times during the 3-year period ending on the date of such distribution,

children.label: ii
children.text: the controlled corporation has been a taxable REIT subsidiary (as defined in section 856()) of the distributing corporation at all times during such period, andl

children.label: iii
children.text: the distributing corporation had control (as defined in section 368(c) applied by taking into account stock owned directly or indirectly, including through one or more corporations or partnerships, by the distributing corporation) of the controlled corporation at all times during such period.
